stories.title: Google Broke Its Promise to Me — Now ICE Has My Data
stories.blurb: The EFF details how Google reversed its commitment to protect user data, handing location history to immigration enforcement despite explicit opt-out settings. If you use any Google services, this is a concrete case study in why trust us doesn't scale. The implications for anyone storing sensitive data in cloud services are immediate and chilling.

stories.title: The Local LLM Ecosystem Doesn't Need Ollama
stories.blurb: A provocative argument that Ollama has become an unnecessary abstraction layer for running local models. The author walks through llama.cpp, vLLM, and other backends that give you more control with less overhead. If you're running local models on your Mac, this is worth reading before your next setup.

stories.title: Cybersecurity Looks Like Proof of Work Now
stories.blurb: Drew Breunig argues that AI-powered attacks have made cybersecurity an arms race where defense now costs more than offense. Treat security like infrastructure, not insurance. If you ship software, this reframes how you should think about every dependency you pull in.

stories.title: Darkbloom — Private Inference on Idle Macs
stories.blurb: Darkbloom turns your idle Mac into a private inference node — run LLM workloads locally with no data leaving your machine. Think of it as a self-hosted alternative to cloud inference that actually respects the silicon sitting on your desk.

stories.title: ChatGPT for Excel
stories.blurb: OpenAI launched a native spreadsheet interface for ChatGPT, blurring the line between conversational AI and structured data tools. It signals that AI companies are coming for the productivity suite, not just the chat window.

stories.title: Cal.com Is Going Closed Source
stories.blurb: Another open-source company pulls up the drawbridge. Is the open-core model fundamentally broken, or did Cal just fail to execute? This matters for anyone who depends on OSS tools that could make the same pivot overnight.

stories.title: Do You Even Need a Database?
stories.blurb: A refreshingly honest examination of when a database is overkill. For small projects, side hustles, and prototypes, flat files, SQLite, or even localStorage might be all you need.

stories.title: I Made a Terminal Pager
stories.blurb: A modern pager that aims to replace less, with syntax highlighting, search, and mouse scrolling out of the box. The kind of small, sharp tool that quietly improves your daily workflow.

stories.title: Libretto — Making AI Browser Automations Deterministic
stories.blurb: Saffron Health open-sourced Libretto, a framework for building browser automations that are reproducible rather than probabilistic. Solves the it worked on my machine problem at the agent level.

stories.title: Agent — Native macOS Coding IDE
stories.blurb: A new native Mac coding environment built around agentic AI workflows. Uses Apple native frameworks instead of Electron, which means it's fast, uses less memory, and integrates with the OS properly.

stories.title: Automate Work with Claude Code Routines
stories.blurb: The official Claude Code documentation for Routines hit Pinboard Popular. Routines let you schedule recurring Claude Code tasks with a prompt file, exactly like this magazine is built.

stories.title: They See Your Photos
stories.blurb: A visual demonstration of what AI can extract from the photos you upload. A visceral reminder that sharing a photo in 2026 means handing over a structured dataset about your life.

stories.title: Google, Microsoft, Meta All Tracking You Even When You Opt Out
stories.byline: Pinboard Popular · tagged: cookies, data-privacy · 404 Media
stories.blurb: An independent audit found that the biggest tech platforms ignore Global Privacy Control signals and set tracking cookies regardless of user preferences, violating California law.

stories.title: Pretty Fish — A Better Mermaid Diagram Editor
stories.byline: Pinboard Popular · tagged: mermaid, diagram, tools
stories.blurb: Mermaid diagrams are everywhere in docs, but the editing experience has always been rough. Pretty Fish gives you a proper visual editor with live preview, drag-and-drop, and export.

stories.title: Steve Klabnik's Jujutsu (jj) Tutorial
stories.blurb: A comprehensive tutorial for jj, the Git-compatible version control system. Offers anonymous branches, automatic rebasing, and undo for everything. From one of the best technical writers in the ecosystem.

stories.title: DaVinci Resolve Photo — Blackmagic's Free Photo Editor
stories.byline: Pinboard Popular · tagged: photo · Blackmagic Design
stories.blurb: Blackmagic added a full photo editing module to DaVinci Resolve, bringing their color science and grading tools to stills for free. The most interesting free option for photographers who don't want to pay Adobe.

stories.title: Apple Frames 4 — Screenshot Framing Tool with CLI
stories.blurb: MacStories released Apple Frames 4, a revamped Shortcut that wraps your screenshots in realistic Apple device frames — now with a CLI for developers. Automate screenshot framing in your build pipeline or documentation workflow.

stories.title: The Git Commands I Run Before Reading Any Code
stories.blurb: A practical walkthrough of the git commands that help you understand a codebase before reading a single line of source. Simple, immediately useful, and separates I cloned it from I understand the shape of this project.

stories.title: Bryan Cantrill: The Peril of Laziness Lost
stories.byline: Pinboard Popular · tagged: programming, llm, ai, design, culture
stories.blurb: Bryan Cantrill argues that AI coding tools are eroding the productive laziness that drove good engineering. A sharp, experience-backed counterpoint to the 10x productivity narrative that everyone building with AI should sit with.

stories.title: The Human Cost of 10x AI Productivity
stories.byline: Pinboard Popular · tagged: ai, llm, softwareengineering, burnout · Denis Stetskov
stories.blurb: AI shifts the bottleneck onto senior engineers whose job is validating what looks correct but may be structurally wrong. Output rises at machine speed while review, trust calibration, and accountability stay stuck at human speed.
